function_explanation: Computes Mie scattering and absorption efficiency spectra for a spherical particle using Lorenz-Mie theory.
function_docstring:
Compute Mie scattering/absorption efficiency spectra using Lorenz-Mie theory.

This function implements a forward model y = A(x) where the input parameters
(refractive index and diameter) are mapped to optical efficiency spectra
using PyMieScatt's MieQ implementation.

Args:
    n_real: Real part of the complex refractive index of the particle.
    k_imag: Imaginary part of the complex refractive index of the particle.
    diameter: Particle diameter in nanometers (nm).
    wavelengths: Array-like of wavelengths in nanometers (nm) at which to
        compute the efficiency spectra. Shape (N,) where N is the number
        of wavelength points.

Returns:
    qsca: Scattering efficiency spectrum as a numpy array of shape (N,),
        where N is the number of wavelengths.
    qabs: Absorption efficiency spectrum as a numpy array of shape (N,).
    qext: Extinction efficiency spectrum as a numpy array of shape (N,).
    g_param: Asymmetry parameter spectrum as a numpy array of shape (N,).
usage_example:
import numpy as np
from agent_forward_operator import forward_operator

# Define particle properties
n_real = 1.5  # Real part of refractive index
k_imag = 0.01  # Imaginary part of refractive index
diameter = 100.0  # Particle diameter in nm

# Define wavelength range
wavelengths = np.linspace(300, 800, 50)  # 50 wavelengths from 300-800 nm

# Compute Mie efficiency spectra
qsca, qabs, qext, g_param = forward_operator(n_real, k_imag, diameter, wavelengths)

print(f"Scattering efficiency at 550 nm: {qsca[25]:.4f}")
print(f"Absorption efficiency at 550 nm: {qabs[25]:.4f}")
